title: The day option accepts invalid values and can send the wrong reminder batch
reasoning: The command contract documents only day 25 or 28, but the implementation casts any input and maps all values below 28 to `reminder` and all values 28 or higher to `follow_up`. A typo such as `--day=2`, `--day=99`, or `--day=abc` can therefore dispatch a real notification batch instead of failing fast.
reproduction: Run `php artisan contributions:remind --day=99`; the command treats it as `follow_up` and processes current-month unpaid contributions even though 99 is outside the documented option contract.
recommendation: Validate the option before querying contributions. Accept only 25 and 28, emit an error, and return `self::FAILURE` for any other explicit value. If no option is provided, either validate that today is one of the scheduled days or require the option in scheduled usage.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The tests cover only the two valid values, 25 and 28, and do not assert that invalid or non-numeric input is rejected without dispatching notifications.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a Pest case that runs `contributions:remind --day=99` and `--day=abc`, asserts a failure exit code, and asserts `Notification::assertNothingSent()`.
minimumFixScope: Add option validation in `SendContributionReminders::handle` before `$type` is derived and before the contribution query runs.
